title: How to Make a Summer Watermelon Slush with Mini Paper Parasols
meta_description: Chill out with a summer watermelon slush and mini paper parasols. Learn how to make this refreshing drink and elevate your presentation with tropical cocktail p
html:
When the temperature climbs, a watermelon slush is the ultimate summer refresher. But why stop at the drink when you can elevate the experience with watermelon slush mini umbrellas for that tropical vibe? These mini paper parasols aren’t just for aesthetics—they’re a fun, functional way to keep your slushie intact while you sip. Here’s how to make the best summer version at home, plus tips for using those colorful cocktail picks to impress your guests.

Why Mini Paper Parasols Make a Big Difference

Mini paper parasols might seem like a small detail, but they’re the perfect finishing touch for summer drinks. The 150-pack cocktail umbrellas from Kitchen Gadget Grove (available in three tropical styles) double as toothpick-style picks, holding together fruit skewers or perching on top of your slushie. They’re disposable, affordable, and instantly transport your drink to a beachside luau. Plus, they secure chunks of watermelon so you don’t lose them to the blender’s vortex.

How to Make a Watermelon Slush (3 Ways)

Start with fresh, seeded watermelon cubes. For the best texture, pre-freeze them—here’s how:

  1. Chop watermelon into 1-inch cubes and freeze for 2–3 hours until firm.
  2. Blend with a splash of water or juice until slushy. For extra flavor, add lime juice, mint, or a splash of coconut water.
  3. Strain for a smoother texture or leave it chunky for a tiki-style slush.

For a creamy version, blend with frozen yogurt or a splash of milk. Need a boost? Add a shot of rum or tequila for a grown-up twist.

Assembling Your Tropical Drink Masterpiece

Once your slush is ready, it’s time to decorate. Grab your 150-pack cocktail umbrellas and:

  • Stick a parasol into a slice of watermelon and place it on top of your cup.
  • Use the picks as skewers for maraschino cherries, pineapple, or mint leaves.
  • Attach a mini umbrella to a regular straw for a hands-free experience.

The three styles (think tiki torch, pineapple, and palm leaf) let you mix and match for a party feel. They’re sturdy enough to hold fruit without bending, so no need to worry about soggy picks.

Pro Tips for Summer Sipper Success

Keep these tricks in mind for the best experience:

  • Prep watermelon cubes in advance and store them in airtight bags for quick slushie sessions.
  • Pair your slush with tropical tunes and a throwback Hawaiian shirt for full tiki effect.
  • Store unused cocktail umbrellas in a dry place. They’re perfect for smoothies, iced teas, or even cake pops at your next party.

FAQ

Can I reuse the paper parasols?

No—these are single-use picks best suited for one-time drink garnishing. But with 150 in a pack, you’ll have plenty for multiple events.

Are the cocktail umbrellas safe for kids?

Absolutely! They’re made from food-safe paper and have blunt tips for easy use with children’s drinks.

Do these picks come in colors besides tropical themes?

This pack focuses on tiki, luau, and tropical styles, but they’re sold as-is for maximum party versatility.
